Show me a breakdown of our customers by:
-- Customer Segmentation by Relationship Type and Customer Type
WITH segmentation AS (
    SELECT
        CustomerTypeId,
        CustomerRelationshipTypeId,
        IsActive,
        COUNT(*) AS CustomerCount
    FROM customer.Customer
    WHERE CustomerRelationshipTypeId IS NOT NULL
      AND CustomerTypeId IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY CustomerTypeId, CustomerRelationshipTypeId, IsActive
),
totals AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS TotalCustomers FROM customer.Customer
)
SELECT
    s.CustomerTypeId AS [Customer Type],
    s.CustomerRelationshipTypeId AS [Relationship Tier],
    SUM(s.CustomerCount) AS [Total Customers],
    SUM(CASE WHEN s.IsActive = true THEN s.CustomerCount ELSE 0 END) AS [Active],
    SUM(CASE WHEN s.IsActive = false THEN s.CustomerCount ELSE 0 END) AS [Inactive],
    ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN s.IsActive = true THEN s.CustomerCount ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / SUM(s.CustomerCount), 1) AS [Active %],
    ROUND(SUM(s.CustomerCount) * 100.0 / MAX(t.TotalCustomers), 1) AS [% of All Customers]
FROM segmentation s
CROSS JOIN totals t
GROUP BY s.CustomerTypeId, s.CustomerRelationshipTypeId
ORDER BY [Total Customers] DESC
I need to analyze our top-performing cus:
-- Top 20 Customers by Total Revenue
WITH customer_revenue AS (
    SELECT
        c.CustomerId,
        c.FirstName,
        c.LastName,
        c.CustomerTypeId,
        c.CustomerRelationshipTypeId,
        COUNT(o.OrderId) AS TotalOrders,
        ROUND(SUM(o.OrderTotal), 2) AS TotalRevenue,
        ROUND(AVG(o.OrderTotal), 2) AS AvgOrderValue,
        MIN(o.OrderDate) AS FirstOrderDate,
        MAX(o.OrderDate) AS LastOrderDate
    FROM customer.Customer c
    INNER JOIN sales.Order o ON c.CustomerId = o.CustomerId
    WHERE o.OrderStatus = 'Completed'
    GROUP BY c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName, c.CustomerTypeId, c.CustomerRelationshipTypeId
)
SELECT
    FirstName,
    LastName,
    CustomerTypeId AS [Customer Type],
    CustomerRelationshipTypeId AS [Tier],
    TotalRevenue AS [Total Revenue],
    TotalOrders AS [Order Count],
    AvgOrderValue AS [Avg Order Value],
    FirstOrderDate AS [First Order],
    LastOrderDate AS [Last Order]
FROM customer_revenue
ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC
LIMIT 20
What are our monthly sales trends? Show:
-- Monthly Sales Trends
SELECT
    YEAR(OrderDate) AS [Year],
    MONTH(OrderDate) AS [Month],
    COUNT(OrderId) AS [Order Count],
    ROUND(SUM(OrderTotal), 2) AS [Total Revenue],
    ROUND(AVG(OrderTotal), 2) AS [Avg Order Value],
    COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerId) AS [Unique Customers]
FROM sales.Order
WHERE OrderStatus = 'Completed'
GROUP BY YEAR(OrderDate), MONTH(OrderDate)
ORDER BY [Year], [Month]
Show me a seasonal analysis of our sales:
-- Seasonal Sales Analysis by Product Category
WITH seasonal_orders AS (
    SELECT
        CASE
            WHEN MONTH(o.OrderDate) IN (12, 1, 2) THEN 'Winter'
            WHEN MONTH(o.OrderDate) IN (3, 4, 5) THEN 'Spring'
            WHEN MONTH(o.OrderDate) IN (6, 7, 8) THEN 'Summer'
            ELSE 'Fall'
        END AS Season,
        ol.ProductName,
        o.OrderId,
        o.CustomerId,
        ol.LineTotal,
        ol.Quantity
    FROM sales.Order o
    JOIN sales.OrderLine ol ON o.OrderId = ol.OrderId
    WHERE o.OrderStatus = 'Completed'
),
total_revenue AS (
    SELECT SUM(OrderTotal) AS GrandTotal FROM sales.Order WHERE OrderStatus = 'Completed'
)
SELECT
    s.Season,
    COUNT(DISTINCT s.OrderId) AS [Orders],
    ROUND(SUM(s.LineTotal), 2) AS [Revenue],
    ROUND(AVG(s.LineTotal), 2) AS [Avg Line Value],
    COUNT(DISTINCT s.CustomerId) AS [Unique Customers],
    ROUND(SUM(s.LineTotal) * 100.0 / MAX(t.GrandTotal), 1) AS [Revenue %]
FROM seasonal_orders s
CROSS JOIN total_revenue t
GROUP BY s.Season
ORDER BY [Revenue] DESC
Which products are generating the most r:
-- Top 15 Products by Revenue with Profitability
WITH product_sales AS (
    SELECT
        p.ProductID,
        p.ProductName,
        p.CategoryName,
        p.StandardCost,
        ol.Quantity,
        ol.UnitPrice,
        (ol.Quantity * ol.UnitPrice) AS LineRevenue,
        (ol.Quantity * ol.UnitPrice) - (ol.Quantity * p.StandardCost) AS LineProfit,
        o.CustomerId
    FROM product.Product p
    JOIN sales.OrderLine ol ON p.ProductID = ol.ProductId
    JOIN sales.Order o ON ol.OrderId = o.OrderId
    WHERE o.OrderStatus = 'Completed'
)
SELECT
    ProductName AS [Product],
    CategoryName AS [Category],
    ROUND(SUM(LineRevenue), 2) AS [Total Revenue],
    SUM(Quantity) AS [Units Sold],
    COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerId) AS [Unique Customers],
    ROUND(SUM(LineProfit), 2) AS [Gross Profit],
    ROUND(SUM(LineProfit) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(LineRevenue), 0), 1) AS [Margin %]
FROM product_sales
GROUP BY ProductID, ProductName, CategoryName
ORDER BY [Total Revenue] DESC
LIMIT 15
How does revenue break down by product c:
-- Revenue and Margin by Product Category
WITH category_sales AS (
    SELECT
        p.CategoryName,
        ol.Quantity,
        ol.UnitPrice,
        (ol.Quantity * ol.UnitPrice) AS LineRevenue,
        (ol.Quantity * ol.UnitPrice) - (ol.Quantity * p.StandardCost) AS LineProfit,
        o.OrderId,
        o.CustomerId
    FROM product.Product p
    JOIN sales.OrderLine ol ON p.ProductID = ol.ProductId
    JOIN sales.Order o ON ol.OrderId = o.OrderId
    WHERE o.OrderStatus = 'Completed'
),
total AS (
    SELECT SUM(LineRevenue) AS GrandTotal FROM category_sales
)
SELECT
    cs.CategoryName AS [Category],
    COUNT(DISTINCT cs.OrderId) AS [Orders],
    SUM(cs.Quantity) AS [Units Sold],
    ROUND(SUM(cs.LineRevenue), 2) AS [Total Revenue],
    ROUND(AVG(cs.UnitPrice), 2) AS [Avg Unit Price],
    ROUND(SUM(cs.LineProfit) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(cs.LineRevenue), 0), 1) AS [Avg Margin %],
    ROUND(SUM(cs.LineRevenue) * 100.0 / MAX(t.GrandTotal), 1) AS [Revenue Share %]
FROM category_sales cs
CROSS JOIN total t
GROUP BY cs.CategoryName
ORDER BY [Total Revenue] DESC
What is the current inventory status? Sh:
-- Inventory Status - Products at or Below Reorder Point
SELECT
    ProductName AS [Product],
    ProductCategory AS [Category],
    WarehouseLocation AS [Warehouse],
    CurrentStock AS [Current Stock],
    ReservedStock AS [Reserved],
    AvailableStock AS [Available],
    SafetyStockLevel AS [Safety Stock],
    ReorderPoint AS [Reorder Point],
    MaxStockLevel AS [Max Capacity],
    ROUND(AverageCost, 2) AS [Avg Unit Cost],
    Status AS [Stock Status]
FROM inventory.Inventory
WHERE Status IN ('Low Stock', 'Out of Stock')
   OR CurrentStock <= ReorderPoint
ORDER BY
    CASE Status
        WHEN 'Out of Stock' THEN 1
        WHEN 'Low Stock' THEN 2
        ELSE 3
    END,
    CurrentStock ASC
Show me all open and in-transit purchase:
-- Open Purchase Orders (Pending and In Transit)
SELECT
    po.PurchaseOrderNumber AS [PO Number],
    po.SupplierName AS [Supplier],
    po.OrderDate AS [Order Date],
    po.ExpectedDeliveryDate AS [Expected Delivery],
    po.ActualDeliveryDate AS [Actual Delivery],
    po.Status AS [PO Status],
    po.Priority AS [Priority],
    po.DeliveryLocation AS [Warehouse],
    ROUND(po.TotalOrderValue, 2) AS [PO Value],
    COUNT(poi.PurchaseOrderItemID) AS [Line Items],
    SUM(poi.QuantityOrdered) AS [Total Units Ordered],
    SUM(poi.QuantityReceived) AS [Units Received]
FROM inventory.PurchaseOrders po
LEFT JOIN inventory.PurchaseOrderItems poi ON po.PurchaseOrderID = poi.PurchaseOrderID
WHERE po.Status IN ('Pending', 'Shipped')
GROUP BY
    po.PurchaseOrderNumber, po.SupplierName, po.OrderDate,
    po.ExpectedDeliveryDate, po.ActualDeliveryDate, po.Status,
    po.Priority, po.DeliveryLocation, po.TotalOrderValue
ORDER BY po.ExpectedDeliveryDate ASC
What are the active supply chain disrupt:
-- Active and Monitored Supply Chain Disruption Events
SELECT
    EventName AS [Event],
    DisruptionType AS [Type],
    Severity AS [Severity],
    AlertLevel AS [Alert Level],
    Status AS [Status],
    GeographicArea AS [Affected Area],
    StartDate AS [Start Date],
    EndDate AS [End Date],
    ProductCategory AS [Category Impact],
    ImpactLevel AS [Impact Level],
    DeliveryDelay AS [Delay (Days)],
    ROUND(CostIncrease, 2) AS [Cost Increase %],
    ROUND(EstimatedRevenueImpact, 2) AS [Est. Revenue Impact],
    AlternativeAction AS [Mitigation Action]
FROM supplychain.SupplyChainEvents
WHERE Status IN ('Active', 'Monitoring')
ORDER BY
    CASE Severity
        WHEN 'Critical' THEN 1
        WHEN 'High' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Medium' THEN 3
        ELSE 4
    END,
    CASE AlertLevel
        WHEN 'Red' THEN 1
        WHEN 'Orange' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Yellow' THEN 3
        ELSE 4
    END
Which customer segments buy which produc:
-- Revenue by Customer Segment and Product Category
WITH segment_category AS (
    SELECT
        c.CustomerRelationshipTypeId,
        p.CategoryName,
        SUM(ol.LineTotal) AS SegmentCategoryRevenue,
        SUM(ol.Quantity) AS UnitsSold,
        COUNT(DISTINCT o.CustomerId) AS UniqueCustomers
    FROM customer.Customer c
    JOIN sales.Order o ON c.CustomerId = o.CustomerId
    JOIN sales.OrderLine ol ON o.OrderId = ol.OrderId
    JOIN product.Product p ON ol.ProductId = p.ProductID
    WHERE o.OrderStatus = 'Completed'
    GROUP BY c.CustomerRelationshipTypeId, p.CategoryName
),
segment_totals AS (
    SELECT CustomerRelationshipTypeId, SUM(SegmentCategoryRevenue) AS SegmentTotal
    FROM segment_category
    GROUP BY CustomerRelationshipTypeId
)
SELECT
    sc.CustomerRelationshipTypeId AS [Customer Tier],
    sc.CategoryName AS [Product Category],
    ROUND(sc.SegmentCategoryRevenue, 2) AS [Revenue],
    sc.UnitsSold AS [Units Sold],
    sc.UniqueCustomers AS [Customers],
    ROUND(sc.SegmentCategoryRevenue * 100.0 / st.SegmentTotal, 1) AS [% of Tier Revenue]
FROM segment_category sc
JOIN segment_totals st ON sc.CustomerRelationshipTypeId = st.CustomerRelationshipTypeId
ORDER BY sc.CustomerRelationshipTypeId, [Revenue] DESC
Show me the full order-to-payment lifecy:
-- Order to Invoice to Payment Reconciliation
SELECT
    o.OrderNumber AS [Order Number],
    o.OrderDate AS [Order Date],
    o.OrderStatus AS [Order Status],
    ROUND(o.OrderTotal, 2) AS [Order Total],
    o.PaymentMethod AS [Payment Method],
    i.InvoiceNumber AS [Invoice Number],
    i.InvoiceStatus AS [Invoice Status],
    ROUND(i.TotalAmount, 2) AS [Invoice Amount],
    i.DueDate AS [Due Date],
    p.PaymentDate AS [Payment Date],
    ROUND(p.PaymentAmount, 2) AS [Payment Amount],
    p.PaymentStatus AS [Payment Status]
FROM sales.Order o
LEFT JOIN finance.invoice i ON o.OrderId = i.OrderId
LEFT JOIN finance.payment p ON i.InvoiceId = p.InvoiceId
WHERE o.OrderStatus = 'Completed'
ORDER BY o.OrderDate DESC
LIMIT 50
What is our supplier network? Show me al:
-- Supplier Network Overview
SELECT
    s.SupplierName AS [Supplier],
    s.SupplierType AS [Type],
    s.Status AS [Status],
    s.ProductCategory AS [Category Coverage],
    s.LeadTimeDays AS [Lead Time (Days)],
    s.ReliabilityScore AS [Reliability Score],
    s.Location AS [Location],
    COUNT(ps.ProductSupplierID) AS [Products Supplied],
    ROUND(AVG(ps.WholesaleCost), 2) AS [Avg Wholesale Cost]
FROM supplychain.Suppliers s
LEFT JOIN supplychain.ProductSuppliers ps ON s.SupplierID = ps.SupplierID
    AND ps.Status = 'Active'
GROUP BY
    s.SupplierID, s.SupplierName, s.SupplierType, s.Status,
    s.ProductCategory, s.LeadTimeDays, s.ReliabilityScore, s.Location
ORDER BY s.SupplierType, s.ProductCategory
How do financial account balances look a:
-- Financial Account Balance Summary
SELECT
    AccountType AS [Account Type],
    AccountStatus AS [Status],
    COUNT(*) AS [Account Count],
    ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(Balance, 0)), 2) AS [Total Balance],
    ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Balance, 0)), 2) AS [Avg Balance],
    ROUND(MAX(Balance), 2) AS [Max Balance],
    ROUND(MIN(Balance), 2) AS [Min Balance]
FROM finance.account
GROUP BY AccountType, AccountStatus
ORDER BY AccountType, [Total Balance] DESC
Show me the demand forecast for the next:
-- Upcoming Quarter Demand Forecast
SELECT
    ProductName AS [Product],
    ProductCategory AS [Category],
    ForecastDate AS [Forecast Date],
    ForecastPeriod AS [Period],
    PredictedDemand AS [Predicted Demand],
    BaselineDemand AS [Baseline Demand],
    ROUND(ConfidenceLevel, 1) AS [Confidence %],
    ROUND(SeasonalMultiplier, 2) AS [Seasonal Multiplier],
    TrendDirection AS [Trend],
    MethodUsed AS [Forecast Method]
FROM inventory.DemandForecast
WHERE ForecastDate >= CURRENT_DATE()
  AND ForecastDate < DATE_ADD(CURRENT_DATE(), 90)
ORDER BY
    CASE TrendDirection WHEN 'Growing' THEN 1 WHEN 'Stable' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END,
    PredictedDemand DESC
Give me a complete inventory vs demand c:
-- Inventory vs Recent Sales Velocity
WITH recent_sales AS (
    SELECT
        ol.ProductName,
        SUM(ol.Quantity) AS UnitsSold30Days,
        COUNT(DISTINCT o.OrderId) AS Orders30Days
    FROM sales.OrderLine ol
    JOIN sales.Order o ON ol.OrderId = o.OrderId
    WHERE o.OrderDate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 30)
      AND o.OrderStatus = 'Completed'
    GROUP BY ol.ProductName
)
SELECT
    inv.ProductName AS [Product],
    inv.ProductCategory AS [Category],
    inv.WarehouseLocation AS [Warehouse],
    inv.CurrentStock AS [Current Stock],
    inv.AvailableStock AS [Available],
    inv.ReorderPoint AS [Reorder Point],
    inv.Status AS [Stock Status],
    COALESCE(rs.UnitsSold30Days, 0) AS [Units Sold Last 30 Days],
    CASE
        WHEN COALESCE(rs.UnitsSold30Days, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
        ELSE ROUND(inv.AvailableStock / (rs.UnitsSold30Days / 30.0), 0)
    END AS [Est. Days of Stock Remaining]
FROM inventory.Inventory inv
LEFT JOIN recent_sales rs ON inv.ProductName = rs.ProductName
ORDER BY [Est. Days of Stock Remaining] ASC NULLS LAST
